description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. FLLN 1010 or FLLN 1013. Departmental approval. Introduction to the knowledge and skills necessary for reading classical Latin.  Fulfills College of Liberal Arts core world language requirement.

title: INTERMEDIATE LATIN I
description: LEC. 4. Pr. FLLN 1020 or FLLN 1023. Four years of high school Latin or Departmental approval. Review of classical Latin grammar with reading of selections from Latin literature.  Fall.

title: INTERMEDIATE LATIN II
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. FLLN 2010 .  or Departmental approval.. Continuation of FLLN 2010 .  Review of classical Latin grammar with reading of  selections from Latin literature. Fulfills the College of Liberal Arts world language core requirement.

title: LATIN LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLLN 2010 or Departmental approval. Advanced readings in Latin prose and poetry.  Course may be repeated with change in topic.

title: ROMAN LITERATURE AND CULTURE IN TRANSLATION
description: LEC. 3. Roman cultural practices and ideology with a focus on literary evidence.  Readings in English. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: DIRECTED STUDIES IN LATIN LITERATURE
description: IND. Pr., Departmental approval. Independent study of Latin texts.  Topic proposed by student in conjunction with faculty advisor.  Course may be repeated with change in topic.

title: ELEMENTARY JAPANESE ABROAD
description: LEC. Course work at the elementary level take on an approved Study Abroad program.  Learning modern Japanese listening, writing, and reading  in an integrated manner through an approved Study Abroad program. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 10 credit hours.

title: ELEMENTARY JAPANESE I
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Fundamentals of Japanese.  Stress on language skills with progressive emphasis on conversation; exposure to Japanese civilization.  Fall.

title: ELEMENTARY JAPANESE II
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. FLJP 1010 or Departmental approval. Stress on language skills; structural review and composition: readings in Japanese literature and exposure to Japanese culture and civilization.

title: INTERMEDIATE JAPANESE ABROAD
description: AAB/LEC. Pr. FLJP 1020 or Departmental approval. Continued development of proficiency in modern Japanese through an approved Study Abroad program; focus on speaking, listening, writing, and reading within cultural contexts.  Students should consult with the Spanish undergraduate director for an estimation of credit prior to going abroad. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 10 credit hours.

title: INTERMEDIATE JAPANESE I
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. FLJP 1020 or Departmental approval. Stress on language skills; structural review and composition; readings in Japanese literature; and exposure to Japanese culture and civilization.  Spring.

title: INTERMEDIATE JAPANESE II
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. FLJP 2010 or Departmental approval. Continuation of FLJP 2010 .  Language skills; structural review and composition; readings in Japanese literature; and exposure to Japanese culture and civilization.  Spring.

title: ADVANCED JAPANESE ABROAD
description: AAB/LEC. Pr. FLJP 1020 or Departmental approval. Continued development of proficiency in modern Japanese through an approved Study Abroad program; focus on speaking, listening, writing, and reading within cultural contexts. Students should consult with the Spanish undergraduate director for an estimation of credit prior to going abroad. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 10 credit hours.

title: JAPANESE COMPOSITION AND CONVERSATION
description: AAB. Pr. FLJP 2010 or Departmental approval. Intensive practice of written and spoken Japanese, based on contemporary social situations and texts. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: JAPANESE COMPOSITION AND CONVERSATION II
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLJP 3010 . Additional intensive practice of written and spoken Japanese, based on contemporary social situations and texts.

title: JAPANESE CINEMA
description: LEC. 3. Introduction to Japanese films, with particular focus on representations of history from the 1930s to the present. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: EXTENSIVE READING: TADOKU
description: LEC. 1.  SU. Pr. FLJP 1010 . Development of Japanese reading skills, the acquisition of vocabulary and grammar and understandings of culture and context. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 3 credit hours. May need instructor’s approval. Students do not have to be currently enrolled in a Japanese language course.

title: TOPICS IN JAPANESE LITERATURE AND CULTURE
description: AAB/LEC. Critical study of specific Japanese literary and cultural topics. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: SURVEY OF MODERN JAPANESE LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Introduction to major works of modern Japanese literature translated into English, from the late 19th century to present.

title: SURVEY OF TRADITIONAL JAPANESE LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Introduction to major works of traditional Japanese literature translated into English, from Early (to 794 CE) to Edo (1600-1868) period.

title: DIRECTED STUDY IN JAPANESE
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLJP 2020 . Directed study in area of special interest for the superior student in Japanese. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: ELEMENTARY RUSSIAN I
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Fundamentals of Russian.  Language skills; progressive emphasis on conversation; exposure to Russian culture and civilization.

title: ELEMENTARY RUSSIAN II
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. FLRU 1010 or FLRU 1013. Fundamentals of Russian.  Stress on language skills; progressive emphasis on conversation; exposure to Russian culture and civilization.  Fulfills College of Liberal Arts world language core requirement.

title: ELEMENTARY SPANISH ABROAD
description: AAB/FLD. Pr., Departmental approval.  Course work at the elementary level in an approved Study Abroad program credit may substitute for required 1000-level courses in Spanish. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 10 credit hours.

title: ELEMENTARY SPANISH I
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Basic language skills stressed with progressive emphasis on conversation.  Exposure to Hispanic civilization.  For students with less than two years of high school Spanish.

title: HONORS ELEMENTARY SPANISH I
description: LEC. 5. Basic language skills stressed with progressive emphasis on conversation. Exposure to Hispanic civilization. For students with less than two years of high school Spanish.

title: ELEMENTARY SPANISH II
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. LSPA score of 0241 or FLSP 1010 or FLSP 1013. or acceptable score on FL placement test. Fundamentals of Spanish language skills stressed with progressive emphasis on conversation.  Exposure to Hispanic civilization.  Fulfills College of Liberal Arts world language core requirement.

title: HONORS ELEMENTARY SPANISH II
description: LEC. 5. Pr. LSPA score of 0241 or FLSP 1010 or FLSP 1013. or acceptable score on FL placement test. Fundamentals of Spanish language skills stressed with progressive emphasis on conversation. Exposure to Hispanic civilization. Fulfills College of Liberal Arts world language core requirement.

title: READING PROFICIENCY IN SPANISH
description: LEC. 3.  SU. Pr., Departmental approval. Instruction to enable graduate students to read and understand scholarly material in Spanish related to their field of study.  May not be used to satisfy undergraduate language requirements. Spring.

title: INTERMEDIATE SPANISH ABROAD
description: AAB/FLD. Pr., Departmental approval. Course work at the intermediate level taken on an approved Study Abroad program.  Students should consult with the Spanish undergraduate director for an estimation of credit prior to going abroad. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 10 credit hours.

title: INTERMEDIATE SPANISH I
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. LSPA score of 0325 or FLSP 1020 . or acceptable score on FL placement test or Departmental approval. Review of grammatical structures, development of reading, writing, and conversation skills, in addition to increased understanding of Hispanic cultures. Fall, Spring.

title: HONORS INTERMEDIATE SPANISH I
description: LEC. 2. LAB. 2. Pr. Honors College. FLSP 1020 or LSPA minimum score of 325. Review of grammatical structures, development of reading and writing skills, and increased understanding of Hispanic cultures.

title: INTERMEDIATE SPANISH II
description: LEC. 3. LAB. 2. Pr. LSPA score of 0372 or FLSP 2010 or FLSP 2017 . or acceptable score on FL placement test. Continued review of grammatical structures, development of reading, writing, and conversation skills, in addition to increased understanding of Hispanic cultures. Fall, Spring.

title: HONORS INTERMEDIATE SPANISH II
description: LEC. 2. LAB. 2. Pr. Honors College. FLSP 2010 or FLSP 2013 or FLSP 2017 or LSPA minimum score of 372. Continued review of grammatical structures, development of reading and writing skills, and increased understanding of Hispanic cultures.

title: JUNIOR ADVANCED SPANISH ABROAD
description: AAB/FLD. Pr., Departmental approval. Course work at the junior or advanced level taken on an approved Study Abroad program.  Students should consult with the Spanish undergraduate director for an estimation of credit prior to going abroad. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 10 credit hours.

title: SPANISH PHONETICS
description: LEC. 45, AAB/LEC. 0. Pr. FLSP 3060 or FLSP 3063. Training in practical phonetics with an emphasis on pronunciation correctives.  Fall, Spring.

title: SPANISH SYNTAX
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 2020 or FLSP 2027 . or exam. In this course, the function of the different parts of speech (e.g., prepositions, adverbs, determiners, pronouns, verbs, conjunctions) will be examined, and students will learn to diagram simple and complex hierarchical sentence structure using syntactic trees.

title: COMMUNICATIVE SKILLS IN SPANISH I
description: LEC. 3. Pr. LSPA score of 0428 or FLSP 2020 or FLSP 2027 . The first in a two semester sequence designed to improve speaking and writing, with additional emphasis placed on the acquisition of vocabulary and grammar, listening and reading comprehension. Uses literary and cultural texts to develop cultural awareness.

title: COMMUNICATIVE SKILLS IN SPANISH II
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 or FLSP 3063. The second in a two semester sequence designed to improve speaking and writing, with additional emphasis placed on the acquisition of vocabulary and grammar, listening and reading comprehension. Uses literary and cultural texts to develop cultural awareness.

title: INTRO TO CULTURAL ANALYSIS
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 or FLSP 3063. A general introduction to the analysis of Hispanic societies through their diverse cultural practices, local customs, and languages. This class explores how were Hispanic societies formed, the effects of globalization, and their diverse cultural and national identities. Spring.

title: SPANISH FOR HEALTH PROFESSIONS
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 or FLSP 3063. Advanced Spanish for students interested in healthcare careers. Designed to improve spoken and written communicative skills in a Spanish-speaking environment.

title: HONORS SPANISH FOR HEALTH PROFESSIONS
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 or FLSP 3063. Advanced Spanish for students interested in healthcare careers. Designed to improve spoken and written communicative skills in a Spanish-speaking environment.

title: INTRODUCTION TO HISPANIC LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 or FLSP 3063. Study of literary genres, rhetorical figures, and other critical concepts. Literary analysis of Spanish and Spanish-American texts. Fall.

title: CULTURES OF SPAIN
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3080 or FLSP 3083 or FLSP 3100 . An introduction to the social and cultural diversity of Spain. This class explores the ethnic and linguistic diversity of the country, the historical events that forged contemporary Spain, and the cultural plurality of the country.

title: TOPICS IN HISPANIC FILM
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3080 or FLSP 3083 or FLSP 3100 . Study of film as a window into Hispanic cultures, both Spanish and Spanish American. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: TOPICS IN HISPANIC MUSIC
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3080 or FLSP 3083 or FLSP 3100 . Study of the interrelationship of Hispanic music and Spanish and Spanish-American cultures. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: TOPICS IN HISPANIC MEDIA
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3080 or FLSP 3083 or FLSP 3100 . Develops students' cultural awareness through a series of written assignments organized around major journalistic and academic genres. We will investigate contemporary issues as presented in the media of Spain, Latin America and U.S. Latino communities. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: LANGUAGES ACROSS THE CURRICULUM SEMINAR IN SPANISH
description: LEC. 1. Pr. FLSP 2010 or FLSP 2017 or Departmental approval. Language component with readings and in-class discussions to complement a lecture course in English and in a discipline other than Spanish.  Parallel enrollment is recommended. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 9 credit hours.

title: CULTURES OF SPANISH AMERICA
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3080 or FLSP 3083 or FLSP 3100 . An introduction to the social and cultural diversity of Spanish America. This class explores the cultural, ethnic and linguistic diversity of the Spanish American countries, and the historical events that forged them.

title: HISPANIC CULTURES IN THE U. S.
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3080 or FLSP 3083 or FLSP 3100 . An introduction to the social and cultural diversity of Hispanic U.S.  This class explores the diversity in ethnicity, traditions, and arts of Hispanics in the country, the historical events that forged such diversity, and their cultural plurality.

title: SPANISH TRANSLATION AND INTERPRETATION
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3070 . Introduction to the techniques of English/Spanish and Spanish/English translation in a commercial environment, including correspondence, technical documents, advertising and oral translation.  Fall.

title: DIRECTED STUDY IN SPANISH
description: IND. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 . Departmental approval. Development of an advanced-level of Spanish proficiency in listening,  speaking, reading, and writing. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: SENIOR ADVANCED SPANISH ABROAD
description: AAB/FLD. Pr., Departmental approval. Course work at the senior/advanced level taken on an approved Study Abroad program. Students should consult with the Spanish undergraduate director for an estimation of credit prior to going abroad. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 9 credit hours.

title: ORAL PROFICIENCY IN SPANISH
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 . Proficiency oriented course designed to further develop speaking and comprehension skills in Spanish in a variety of topics.

title: CONTINUING SPANISH SYNTAX
description: AAB/IND. Departmental approval. Continuing practice in Spanish syntax. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 3 credit hours.

title: SPANISH LINGUISTICS
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3070 . This course introduces students to Spanish linguistics and the basic concepts of some of its principal branches. The primary areas include phonetics and phonology, syntax, morphology, semantics, pragmatics, history of the Spanish language, and linguistics as a cognitive science.

title: MASTERPIECES OF SPANISH LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3100 or FLSP 3080 . Major works of Spanish literature from medieval times to the present.

title: TOPICS IN SPANISH LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3100 or FLSP 3080 . Readings in Spanish literature.  Course may be repeated with change in topic.

title: MASTERPIECES OF SPANISH-AMER LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3, AAB/LEC. 0. Pr. FLSP 3100 or FLSP 3080 . Major works of Spanish American literature from colonial times to the present.

title: TOPICS IN SPANISH-AMERICAN LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3, AAB/LEC. 0. Pr. FLSP 3100 or FLSP 3080 . Readings in Spanish-American Literature. Course may be repeated with a change in topic.

title: BUSINESS SPANISH
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3060 . Business vocabulary and terminology, business practices, and cultural influences in the Hispanic world.

title: TOPICS IN HISPANIC COMMERCIAL WORLD
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3070 . Study of an aspect of Spanish business terminology or documentation.  Course may be repeated with change in topic. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: TOPICS IN HISPANIC LITERATURE, CULTURE, AND LINGUISTICS
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3080 or FLSP 3083 or FLSP 3100 . Study of specialized periods or concepts in Hispanic literature, culture, or linguistics. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: PRACTICUM IN SPANISH
description: AAB/PRA. Pr., Departmental approval. Practical work experience related to the major field. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 3 credit hours.

title: SENIOR CAPSTONE
description: IND. 1.  SU. Assessment of language skills through written and oral exams.  Fall, Spring.

title: ADVANCED SPANISH PHONETICS
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3070 . Advanced training in Spanish phonetics with specific course materials determined by needs of students.

title: ADVANCED SPANISH SYNTAX
description: LEC. 3, AAB/LEC. 0. Pr. FLSP 3070 . Advanced training in Spanish syntax and stylistics with specific course materials determined by needs of students.

title: ADVANCED CULTURE AND LITERATURE OF THE SPANISH SPEAKING WORLD
description: LEC. 3. Pr. FLSP 3070 and FLSP 3080 . A grade of C or better in at least 4 courses FLSP 3000 -3999 or departmental approval.  Advanced training in Culture and literature of the Spanish speaking world with specific course materials determined by faculty. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: ADVANCED SPANISH PHONETICS
description: LEC. 3. Pr. At least 4 courses each with a minimum grade of D in FLSP  3000-3999. Four courses or Departmental approval. Advanced training in Spanish phonetics with specific course materials determined by needs of students.

title: ADVANCED SPANISH SYNTAX
description: LEC. 3. Pr. At least 4 courses each with a minimum grade of D in FLSP  3000-3999. Advanced training in Spanish syntax and morphology to carry out syntactic analysis for graduate students and undergraduate students who have previously completed FLSP 3050 .

title: ADVANCED CULTURE AND LITERATURE OF SPANISH SPEAKING WORLD
description: LEC. 3. Advanced training in Culture and literature of the Spanish speaking world with specific course materials determined by faculty. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 6 credit hours.

title: GRADUATE SPANISH ABROAD
description: AAB/FLD. Pr., Departmental approval. Course work at the graduate level taken on an approved Study Abroad program.  Students should consult with the Spanish graduate director for an estimation of credit prior to going abroad. Course may be repeated for a maximum of 9 credit hours.

title: HISTORY OF THE SPANISH LANGUAGE
description: LEC. 3. Diachronic study of the development of the Spanish language from its Latin origins to the present.

title: SPANISH LINGUISTICS
description: LEC. 3. Synchronic study of the Spanish language focusing on phonology, morphology, syntax, and lexicon, taking into consideration dialectal differences.

title: APPLIED SPANISH LINGUISTICS
description: LEC. 3. Critical study of current research in applied linguistics regarding the acquisition of Spanish by non-native speakers, with emphasis on the problems faced by adult English-speaking individuals.

title: LITERARY CRITICISM AND THEORY
description: LEC. 3. Contemporary literary criticism and theory as it relates to Spanish and Spanish-American literature.

title: RESEARCH METHODS
description: LEC. 1.  SU. Methods of scholarly investigation in literary history and criticism.  Credit may not be used to satisfy degree requirements.

title: INTRODUCTION TO COLLEGE LEVEL SPANISH INSTRUCTION
description: LEC. 3. Instruction for GTAs, including critical observation in performance and guidance by a designated supervisory professor.  Required of all students who hold a graduate teaching assistantship.

title: SPANISH MEDIEVAL LITERATURE I
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of medieval Spanish literature through representative texts from the various genres of the period beginning with the  origins of Spanish literature until 1299.

title: SPANISH MEDIEVAL LITERATURE II
description: LEC. 3. Study of medieval Spanish literature through representative texts from the various genres of the period 1300-1500.

title: 16TH-CENTURY SPANISH LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of representative literary works in all genres from around 1492 to the end of the 16th century.

title: 17TH-CENTURY SPANISH LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of representative literary works in all genres in the 17th century with emphasis on Baroque literature.

title: SURVEY OF 18TH AND 19TH-CENTURY SPANISH LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Survey of the major literary, cultural, and historical trends in 18th and 19th century Spanish literature.

title: HISPANIC COLONIAL LITERATURE OF THE UNITED STATES
description: LEC. 3. Literature about the colonial Hispanic exploration and colonization of the United States from the 16th to 19th centuries.

title: 20TH-CENTURY SPANISH LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of 20th-century Peninsular literature from the Generation of 98 to Spanish post-war literature through representative works in all genres.

title: CONTEMPORARY SPANISH LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of contemporary literature from the Spanish Civil War to the present through representative works in all genres.

title: COLONIAL SPANISH-AMERICAN LITERATURE
description: LEC. 3. Study of representative literary genres and authors of Vice Regal America from Spanish transcription of pre-Columbian works to those just prior to the Wars of Independence.

title: SPANISH-AMERICAN POETRY I
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of Spanish-American poetry from 1824 to the first generation of modernism.

title: SPANISH-AMERICAN POETRY II
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of Spanish-American poetry from post-modernism to the present.

title: SPANISH-AMERICAN POST-COLONIAL PROSE TEXTS TO THE NEW NARRATIVE
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of representative essayists and fiction writers of the 19th and 20th centuries predating the New Narrative.

title: THE NEW NARRATIVE IN SPANISH-AMERICAN FICTION: MODERNIST AND POST-MODERNIST TEXTS
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of major works of modernist and post-modernist  fiction that achieved international acclaim during the second half of the 20th century.

title: SPANISH AMERICAN THEATER I
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of the Spanish-American theater, with emphasis on the period prior to 1900.

title: SPANISH AMERICAN THEATER II
description: LEC. 3. Critical and historical study of the Spanish-American theater from 1900 to present.

title: DON QUIJOTE
description: LEC. 3. Critical study of Cervantes' masterpiece.

title: SPECIAL TOPICS IN LINGUISTICS, LITERATURE AND CULTURE
description: AAB. 3. Pr., BA in Spanish or BS in Foreign Language Education in Spanish.  In-depth study of an author or authors and analysis of the cultural milieu influencing their creativity or investigation of a specific linguistic phenomenon in Spanish.  Course may be repeated with a change in topic. Course may be repeated with change in topics.

title: RESEARCH AND THESIS
description: MST. Directed readings and research culminating in a thesis. Course may be repeated with change in topic.
